perturbation.original_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Talent's services under this Agreement, ESSI agrees as follows: A. Equity Payment: ESSI will provide Talent with one-million (1,000,000) shares of restricted common stock issued within ten business days of execution of this Agreement. B. Cash Payments: (1) ESSI will provide monthly payment of Ten Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($10,000) made payable to Talent for Talent's social communications with its followers on popular social media venues, including, but not limited to, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Snap Chat where Talent may communicate about the ESSI product suite ("Social Communication"). The monthly Social Communication schedule will be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI one month in advance of the actual month of communications. Once the monthly Social Communication schedule is set, the volume of Posts, Tweets and Snaps will determine the total amount of monthly compensation available to Talent. ESSI's Social Media Manager will keep track of Talent's activity pursuant to ESSI's Company quarter to determine the amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter. (a) Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Talent's services under this Agreement, ESSI agrees as follows: A. Equity Payment: ESSI will provide Talent with one-million (1,000,000) shares of restricted common stock issued within ten business days of execution of this Agreement. B. Cash Payments: (1) ESSI will provide monthly payment to Talent for Talent's social communications with its followers on popular social media venues, including, but not limited to, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Snap Chat where Talent may communicate about the ESSI product suite ("Social Communication"). The monthly Social Communication schedule will be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI one month in advance of the actual month of communications. Once the monthly Social Communication schedule is set, the volume of Posts, Tweets and Snaps will determine the total amount of monthly compensation available to Talent. ESSI's Social Media Manager will keep track of Talent's activity pursuant to ESSI's Company quarter to determine the amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter. (a) Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific monthly payment amount of Ten Thousand Dollars ($10,000), the contract now lacks a clearly defined cash payment amount. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because while the section continues to discuss 'monthly payment' and 'total amount of monthly compensation', the actual monetary value is undefined, leading to potential disputes about the consideration Talent is owed. The later reference to 'amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter' becomes ambiguous without the base amount.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Talent's services under this Agreement, ESSI agrees as follows: A. Equity Payment: ESSI will provide Talent with one-million (1,000,000) shares of restricted common stock issued within ten business days of execution of this Agreement. B. Cash Payments: (1) ESSI will provide monthly payment to Talent for Talent's social communications with its followers on popular social media venues, including, but not limited to, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Snap Chat where Talent may communicate about the ESSI product suite ("Social Communication"). The monthly Social Communication schedule will be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI one month in advance of the actual month of communications. Once the monthly Social Communication schedule is set, the volume of Posts, Tweets and Snaps will determine the total amount of monthly compensation available to Talent. ESSI's Social Media Manager will keep track of Talent's activity pursuant to ESSI's Company quarter to determine the amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter. (a) Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.justification: The omission of the specific monthly payment amount ($10,000) in the 'Changed Text' creates a contradiction with the rest of the section. While the section continues to refer to 'monthly payment', 'total amount of monthly compensation', and 'amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter', the lack of a defined monetary value makes these references ambiguous and unenforceable. The Changed Text fails to state a definitive amount for consideration which is a core requirement and expectation of the 'Contradicted Text' and overall contract. This fits the definition of an Omissions - In Text Contradiction because it removes essential language, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections of the contract itself.

perturbation.original_text: 9.Review, Approval, and Ownership of Commercial Placements. All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Talent. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved. Any such usage featuring Talent in the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of ESSI; however, ESSI shall have the right to use said Commercial Placements and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Talent may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Talent's work in Talent's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Talent's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.Review, Approval, and Ownership of Commercial Placements. All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior approval of Talent. Any such usage featuring Talent in the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of ESSI; however, ESSI shall have the right to use said Commercial Placements and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Talent may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Talent's work in Talent's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Talent's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved', the contract now lacks a specified timeframe for Talent's approval. This creates an in-text contradiction because, without a deadline, the initial statement requiring 'prior approval' becomes ambiguous and unenforceable. The absence of a defined period introduces uncertainty regarding how long ESSI must wait for approval before proceeding, potentially leading to delays and disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.Review, Approval, and Ownership of Commercial Placements. All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior approval of Talent. Any such usage featuring Talent in the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of ESSI; however, ESSI shall have the right to use said Commercial Placements and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Talent may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Talent's work in Talent's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Talent's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the five-business-day deadline for approval, as described in the perturbation explanation, creates a contradiction with the requirement for 'prior approval' in Section 9 of the unchanged/contradicted text. The omission introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, making the 'prior approval' requirement unenforceable and thus a contradiction. Without a timeframe, ESSI's ability to use Talent Attributes is hindered, directly conflicting with the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Term of Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a thirty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Term of Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date, and shall automatically be extended.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the option for either party to provide a 'thirty-day notice of written termination' to end the agreement, the contract now lacks a clear mechanism for terminating the agreement after the initial one-year term. This omission introduces an in-text contradiction, because although the agreement states that it is for one year, it also specifies that the agreement 'shall automatically be extended', implying it extends indefinitely without an out. This directly conflicts with the initially stated limited one-year term and any implied expectation that the agreement could be ended after that period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Term of Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date, and shall automatically be extended.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for termination with a 30-day notice after the initial one-year term. The changed text removes this termination clause, implying an indefinite extension without a clear exit strategy. This directly contradicts the initially stated limited one-year term as the contradicted text repeats that the agreement 'shall automatically be extended' without the option for either party to terminate the agreement after the initial one year.
